steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens with the narrator's general observation — that every crew member believes he alone is doing all the work — then illustrates it with Harris's view and George's view from a specific boat trip. The correct answer must capture this two-part structure: generalization followed by examples.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To address a particular situation that the narrator finds unexpected: The narrator says he has 'always noticed' this pattern — it is habitual, not unexpected. ✗
  • Choice B — To convey the narrator's confidence that he understands the role expected of him in a group: The text is about Harris's and George's beliefs, not the narrator's own role or self-confidence. ✗
  • Choice C — To examine how the narrator and his friends each contributed to navigating a challenge: The passage describes what each person believed about contributions, not what they actually did. No challenge is being navigated in the excerpt. ✗
  • Choice D — To present the narrator's generalization along with supporting examples from a specific situation: The opening sentence is the generalization; Harris's view and George's view are the specific examples that support it. This matches the structure exactly. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
